title: Iran attacks Diego Garcia base in Indian Ocean
description: Iran launched a missile attack targeting Diego Garcia, a US-UK military base in the British Indian Ocean Territory, approximately 2,500 miles from Iranian territory. The US and Israel stated the attack demonstrated that Iran's missile range exceeded what Tehran had previously acknowledged.
source_excerpt: Iran is accused of launching a missile attack targeting Diego Garcia, a U.S.-U.K. military base in the Indian Ocean, around 2,500 miles from Iranian territory (ABC News).

title: UK expands base authorization for US operations
description: The UK government expanded its authorization for US use of British military bases, now including 'US defensive operations to degrade the missile sites and capabilities being used to attack ships' in the Strait of Hormuz. Bases approved include Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire.
source_excerpt: On 20 March the government said that US use of the bases also includes 'US defensive operations to degrade the missile sites and capabilities being used to attack ships' (UK House of Commons Library).

title: US F-35 makes emergency landing after anti-aircraft hit
description: A US Air Force F-35 fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at an American base in the Middle East after being hit by anti-aircraft fire during a mission over Iran. The incident demonstrated Iran retained some residual air defense capability despite US claims of total air supremacy.
source_excerpt: A U.S. Air Force F-35 fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at an American base in the Middle East after being hit by anti-aircraft fire during a mission (Alma Research Center).

title: Trump issues first Hormuz ultimatum to Iran
description: President Trump threatened to obliterate Iran's power plants if the Strait of Hormuz was not fully reopened within 48 hours. Iran's army responded by threatening to target all US-linked energy infrastructure in the region if attacked. Trump would subsequently extend this deadline multiple times.
source_excerpt: If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS (Al Jazeera).

title: Trump signals US considering winding down operations
description: On Truth Social, Trump wrote the US was 'getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East.' This contradicted his simultaneous Hormuz ultimatum, establishing a pattern of mixed messaging about the war's trajectory.
source_excerpt: We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East with respect to the Terrorist Regime of Iran (NPR).

title: US lifts sanctions on Iranian oil at sea
description: The Trump administration temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships, expected to add approximately 140 million barrels to the global market. The waiver was set to last until April 19, 2026. The administration had already lifted some US sanctions on Russian crude to ease prices.
source_excerpt: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says the decision is expected to quickly add about 140 million barrels to the global oil market (NPR).

title: US deploys A-10s and Apaches signaling reduced threat
description: The US began using A-10 Warthog attack planes and Apache helicopters in combat operations over Iran, indicating the Pentagon assessed Iran's air defense threat had been drastically reduced. These slower, low-altitude aircraft are more vulnerable than stealth platforms used earlier in the war.
source_excerpt: The U.S. is now using Apache helicopters and A-10 Warthog planes to carry out attacks, a clear sign that the military believes the threat from Iranian forces has been drastically reduced (NPR).

title: US-Israel strikes Iranian steelworks; economic targeting shift
description: Airstrikes targeted three major Iranian steelworks, reflecting an apparent shift in US-Israeli strategy toward degrading Iran's economic and industrial base beyond military targets. This marked an escalation from purely military to dual-use economic infrastructure.
source_excerpt: Airstrikes targeted three major Iranian steelworks, reflecting an apparent shift in U.S.-Israeli strategy toward degrading Iran's economic base (ABC News).

title: Iranian drones strike Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia
description: Iranian drones and missiles targeted Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging several American aircraft including an E-3 Sentry AWACS and wounding multiple US service members. More than two dozen US troops were wounded in Iranian attacks on the base over the preceding week.
source_excerpt: Iranian drones and missiles targeted the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging several American aircraft — among them an E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft — and wounding multiple service members (ABC News).

title: Israel announces Lebanon home demolition campaign
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated the IDF would destroy homes in southern Lebanon using the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' from Gaza, to eliminate Hezbollah militants. This signaled a significant escalation of the ground invasion launched March 16.
source_excerpt: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said the Israeli military will destroy homes in southern Lebanon, just as it did in the war-torn Gaza Strip (ABC News).

title: US delivers 15-point peace plan via Pakistan; Iran rejects
description: The US delivered a peace plan through Pakistani mediators demanding Iran shutter its nuclear program, end ballistic missile production, and open the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for discussing sanctions reduction. Iran rejected the plan and offered a counterproposal demanding reparations and sovereignty recognition over Hormuz.
source_excerpt: The US plan demanded that Iran shutter its nuclear program, end ballistic missile production, and open the Strait of Hormuz in return for discussing the potential reduction of sanctions (The National Interest).

title: Trump extends Hormuz deadline to April 6
description: Trump announced he was pausing his threat to destroy Iran's energy infrastructure for 10 days, pushing the deadline to April 6 at 8 PM Eastern. He claimed this was at the Iranian government's request and that talks were 'going very well,' though Iran denied any negotiations were taking place.
source_excerpt: Please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time (Trump, Truth Social).

title: Houthis enter war with missile strikes on Israel
description: Yemen's Houthi rebels launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since the war began, targeting Beersheba. The IDF intercepted one missile. The Houthis vowed to continue attacks until 'the aggression on all resistance fronts stops,' marking a significant conflict expansion after weeks on the sidelines.
source_excerpt: Yemen's Houthi rebels have attacked Israel with a barrage of ballistic missiles, their first such strikes since the United States-Israeli war on Iran began (Al Jazeera).

title: 2,500 US Marines arrive in Middle East
description: The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit, comprising 2,500 Marines, arrived in the Middle East. The deployment was described as part of the effort to open the Strait of Hormuz, fueling speculation about a possible operation to seize Iran's Kharg Island or secure Hormuz by force.
source_excerpt: 2,500 US marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit arrived in the Middle East, intended to be part of the effort to open the Strait of Hormuz (CNN).

title: Israel strikes Iran nuclear sites: Arak and Yazd
description: Israel struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak and the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed the sites were targeted but said no casualties or contamination risk resulted. Israel called the Yazd strike a major blow to Iran's nuclear program.
source_excerpt: Iran's Atomic Energy Organization said the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak and the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province were targeted (PBS/AP).

title: Four-nation ministerial summit in Islamabad
description: Foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened in Islamabad for a two-day diplomatic summit on ending the war. The meeting produced a joint five-point initiative calling for an immediate ceasefire, urgent diplomatic engagement, and restoration of maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.
source_excerpt: Two-day consultations of foreign ministers of Turkiye, Saudi Arabia, Egypt and Pakistan started in Islamabad as the capital turned into the centre of a rapidly forming diplomatic track (Al Jazeera).

title: Pakistan secures Hormuz passage for 20 ships
description: Iran agreed to allow 20 ships under Pakistani flag to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, with two ships crossing daily. Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar called it a 'welcome and constructive gesture' and a 'harbinger of peace.' This represented the first formal safe-passage arrangement since Iran's closure.
source_excerpt: Iran has agreed to allow 20 ships under Pakistani flag to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, two ships will cross the strait daily (CNN).

title: Israel strikes dozens of Iranian defense industry sites
description: Israel announced it had struck dozens of additional Iranian defense industry sites, continuing the systematic degradation of Iran's arms manufacturing capacity. The US reported it had destroyed approximately two-thirds of Iran's arms manufacturing capacity by this point in the war.
source_excerpt: On March 30, Israel announced that it had struck dozens of additional Iranian defense industry sites (RAND).

title: NATO allies block US military access for Iran operations
description: Spain closed its airspace to US military flights related to Iran. Italy denied US bombers landing at Sigonella Air Base in Sicily. France blocked aircraft carrying military supplies to Israel from its airspace. Switzerland and Austria also restricted US military overflights, creating a significant NATO rift.
source_excerpt: Spain closed its airspace to US jets, and Italy denied US military aircraft bound for the Middle East permission to land at a base in Sicily (Bloomberg).

title: Iran parliament approves Hormuz transit toll system
description: Iran's parliamentary commission approved legislation to charge tolls on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, effectively asserting sovereign control over the waterway. Parliamentary security committee head Ebrahim Azizi told Trump the strait would reopen 'but not for you,' declaring '47 years of hospitality are over.'
source_excerpt: State media reported that a parliamentary commission approved tolls on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas route effectively closed by the war (Al Jazeera).

title: IRGC confirms death of naval commander Tangsiri
description: The IRGC confirmed the death of Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the IRGC Navy, who was killed in an Israeli strike. Tangsiri was responsible for Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure operations. Iran's navy launched its 87th wave of regional attacks the same day as a demonstration of continued capability.
source_excerpt: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps confirmed the death of its naval commander, Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, who was killed in an Israeli strike (Al Jazeera).

title: Israel announces occupation to Litani River in Lebanon
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced Israeli forces would occupy Lebanese territory up to the Litani River, approximately 18 miles north of the Israeli border, and block the return of hundreds of thousands of displaced residents. Two UNIFIL peacekeepers were killed the same day.
source_excerpt: Katz says Israeli forces will occupy Lebanese territory up to the Litani River and block the return of hundreds of thousands of displaced residents (ABC News).

title: US gas prices surpass $4 per gallon
description: Average US gasoline prices rose above $4 per gallon for the first time since 2022, driven by the war's disruption of oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude remained volatile around $103-105 per barrel. The IEA warned that April prices were likely to be 'much worse than March.'
source_excerpt: Gas prices eclipse $4 a gallon in the US, the highest since 2022 (AP).

title: Iran International: 4,700 security forces killed
description: Iran International reported that at least 4,700 Iranian security forces personnel had been killed since the start of the war, significantly higher than previously reported figures. The NGO HRANA had documented 3,114 deaths by March 17, including 1,354 civilians and 1,138 confirmed military.
source_excerpt: On 31 March, Iran International reported that at least 4,700 security forces had been killed (Wikipedia/Iran International).

title: Trump addresses nation: war 'nearing completion'
description: Trump delivered his first prime-time address on the war, 33 days after it began. He said the war was 'nearing completion' and projected two to three more weeks of involvement. He threatened to bomb Iran 'back to the stone ages' but offered no new details, diplomatic plans, or a definitive end date.
source_excerpt: Trump used his first address to the nation on the Iran war, telling Americans the war is nearing completion and projected another two or three weeks of involvement (CNN).

title: Trump threatens to leave NATO over allied refusal
description: In an interview with The Telegraph, Trump said he was 'strongly considering' pulling the US out of NATO because allied nations did not join the Iran war. He called NATO a 'paper tiger.' Secretary of State Rubio warned that if NATO was 'just about defending Europe,' it would have to be re-examined.
source_excerpt: Trump said he was strongly considering pulling out of NATO because those nations did not join the war in Iran (NPR).

title: Pakistan-China joint peace initiative launched
description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar visited Beijing for talks with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi following the Islamabad four-nation summit. They produced a joint Chinese-Pakistani peace initiative that was shared with Iran, the US, and regional stakeholders. China endorsed Pakistan's mediation role.
source_excerpt: China has conveyed support to Tehran for Pakistan's mediation efforts and encouraged Iran to engage with the diplomatic process (Al Jazeera).

title: Houthis launch third missile attack on Israel
description: The Houthis carried out a third missile attack on Israel, with the IDF intercepting a missile launched from Yemen. The group claimed to have targeted sensitive Israeli military sites with a barrage of ballistic missiles in coordination with Hezbollah and Iran.
source_excerpt: A third missile attack was carried out by the Houthis on Israel as the IDF stated that it intercepted a missile launched from Yemen (Wikipedia).

title: White House requests $1.5 trillion defense budget
description: The White House released its fiscal 2027 budget blueprint requesting roughly $1.5 trillion for defense, a more than 40% increase over the previous year. It aims to bolster munitions, expand the naval fleet, begin construction on the 'Golden Dome' missile defense system, and provide 5-7% military pay raises.
source_excerpt: The White House is seeking roughly $1.5 trillion for defense as part of a fiscal 2027 budget request, a proposal that would boost military spending to its highest point in modern history (CNN).

title: Iran shoots down US F-15E over Iranian territory
description: Iran shot down a US F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet over southwestern Iran, the first US warplane downed by enemy fire in over 20 years. One crew member was rescued by US special forces; the second was missing. Iran also struck an A-10 Warthog and two Black Hawk helicopters involved in the rescue.
source_excerpt: Iran shot down the F-15E Strike Eagle and the American military was scrambling to find the second aviator after a regional governor offered a bounty for its crew (NBC News).

title: US strikes Tehran-Karaj B1 bridge; civilian infrastructure hit
description: The US bombed the B1 bridge between Tehran and Karaj, killing eight people according to Iranian media. Trump posted video of the strike and threatened bridges and power plants would be next. Iran's FM Araghchi said striking civilian infrastructure 'will not compel Iranians to surrender.'
source_excerpt: Iranian media said eight people were killed in the strike on the B1 Bridge between Tehran and Karaj (NBC News).

title: Trump issues final 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum for April 6
description: Trump warned Iran that 'time is running out' and gave a 48-hour deadline expiring April 6 for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face devastating strikes on power plants and energy infrastructure. Iran's central military command rejected the threat, warning 'the gates of hell will open for you.'
source_excerpt: Time is running out — 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them (Trump, Truth Social via CBS News).

title: Israeli police disperse anti-war protest in Tel Aviv
description: Israeli police broke up an anti-war protest at HaBima Square in Tel Aviv, arresting at least 17 people. The Supreme Court had authorized a protest of 600 people, but police said nearly 1,000 attended. Protesters were pushed and thrown to the ground within approximately 30 minutes.
source_excerpt: Israeli police broke up an anti-war protest in Tel Aviv on Saturday, arresting at least 17 people (CNN).

title: UAE Habshan gas facility suspends operations after attack
description: The UAE's largest natural gas processing facility, Habshan, suspended operations after debris from a projectile interception sparked a fire. Separately, a drone attack set ablaze Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery, which processes nearly 350,000 barrels per day of crude.
source_excerpt: The UAE's largest natural gas processing facility, Habshan, suspended operations after debris from a projectile interception sparked a fire (Fortune).

title: Israel strikes Iranian petrochemical plants
description: Israeli PM Netanyahu confirmed Israel attacked Iran's petrochemical plants after Tehran reported that strikes damaged multiple petrochemical companies. This expanded the targeting of Iran's economic infrastructure beyond oil to include the broader petrochemical sector.
source_excerpt: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed Israel attacked Iran's petrochemical plants, after Tehran reported that strikes damaged multiple petrochemical companies (CNN).

title: Projectile hits Bushehr nuclear power plant grounds
description: An airstrike reportedly hit the grounds of Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant. The IAEA reported Iran informed it that one physical protection staff member was killed by a projectile fragment and a building on site was affected by shockwaves. Neither the US nor Israel confirmed involvement.
source_excerpt: IAEA said that Iran had informed the body that one of the site's physical protection staff members was killed by a projectile fragment and that a building on site was affected (RFE/RL).

title: Houthis claim Ben Gurion Airport attack with cluster missile
description: The Houthis claimed to have targeted Ben Gurion Airport and vital military sites in southern Israel using a cluster missile and several drones. The IDF stated that a missile launched from Yemen fell in an open area, causing no injuries or damages.
source_excerpt: The Houthis claimed to have targeted Ben Gurion Airport and vital military sites in southern Israel using a cluster missile and several drones (Wikipedia).

title: Italy's Meloni publicly disagrees with Trump on war
description: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni, typically a close Trump ally, publicly stated she 'disagrees' with Trump's criticism of European nations and the broader conduct of the war. She traveled to Saudi Arabia and then Qatar to secure alternative energy supplies for Italy amid the economic fallout.
source_excerpt: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni says she disagrees with the US president's criticism of European nations and their stance on the war with Iran (CNN).

title: Second F-15E crew member rescued by SEAL Team 6
description: US Navy SEAL Team 6 extracted the missing weapons systems officer whose F-15E was shot down over Iran on April 3. The officer, an Air Force colonel, had evaded Iranian forces for more than 24 hours in mountainous terrain before commandos reached him. Trump posted 'WE GOT HIM!' on Truth Social.
source_excerpt: The officer had evaded Iranian forces for more than 24 hours in mountainous terrain before commandos reached him Saturday night (Yahoo News).

title: Iran missile strike hits Haifa; war enters Day 36
description: An apparent Iranian ballistic missile struck Haifa, Israel, prompting emergency responders to search for missing people. The ongoing missile campaign continued despite US claims of having destroyed the vast majority of Iran's launchers. Iran maintained 20-40 missile launches per day, down from 400+ on Day 1.
source_excerpt: Israeli emergency responders search for missing people at the site of an apparent Iranian ballistic missile strike on April 5, 2026 in Haifa, Israel (CBS News).

title: Trump signals Tuesday 8 PM escalation deadline
description: Trump posted 'Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time!' on Truth Social and told the Wall Street Journal the US would destroy 'every power plant' in Iran if it doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday evening. He also said bridges and power plants would be targeted, setting up an April 8 escalation point.
source_excerpt: Trump told The Wall Street Journal the U.S. will destroy every power plant in Iran if the country doesn't agree to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday (CBS News).

title: UAE reports nearly 500 ballistic missiles intercepted since war began
description: The UAE reported its air defense systems had engaged 23 ballistic missiles and 56 drones from Iran on April 4 alone. Cumulatively since February 28, UAE defenses had engaged nearly 500 ballistic missiles, 23 cruise missiles, and more than 2,100 drones, with at least 13 deaths and 215+ injuries.
source_excerpt: Since the start of the war, UAE air defenses have engaged nearly 500 ballistic missiles, 23 cruise missiles and more than 2,100 drones (CBS News).

title: US military casualties reach 15 dead, 520+ wounded
description: By early April, at least 15 US service members had been killed in the war (13 confirmed by the Pentagon as of March, with additional casualties from subsequent incidents). More than 520 US personnel had been wounded according to an Intercept analysis, though the Pentagon's official figure stood at 365 wounded in action.
source_excerpt: At least 15 U.S. troops in the Middle East have died since the beginning of the Iran war; more than 520 U.S. personnel have also been injured (The Intercept).

title: Iran cluster warhead strikes Haifa area and Shefa-Amr
description: An Iranian missile with a cluster warhead struck multiple locations in northern Israel, including the Arab town of Shefa-Amr and the Bazan petrochemical facilities in the Haifa area. The use of cluster munitions increased the area of impact and raised risks to civilian populations.
source_excerpt: A missile with a cluster warhead launched from Iran struck multiple locations in the north, including the Arab town of Shefa-Amr and the Bazan facilities in the Haifa area (Alma Research Center).

title: Iran exempts Iraqi ships from Hormuz restrictions
description: Iran's military announced that Iraq would be exempt from shipping restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz, potentially reopening up to 3 million barrels per day of Iraqi oil exports. Combined with the Pakistani passage deal, this represented selective easing while maintaining the broader closure against US allies.
source_excerpt: Iran's military said Saturday that Iraq would be exempt from shipping restrictions in the strait, opening the potential of as much as 3 million barrels a day of Iraqi oil cargoes (Fortune).
